prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Verily, I, Brother Aldric of the Order of Perpetual Ledgers, was summoned by the Most Serene Lords of the Cloud-Castle "Salesforce" to inscribe their Third Quarter Revelations, though mine eyes grew dim at their sorcerous runes.  

Lo, they spake of "recurring revenue streams" as if silver flowed from enchanted fonts without labor of man, and their "churn rate" was measured not in butter but in departed souls vanishing from their digital fiefdoms.  

Methinks their "burn rate" doth alarm them greatly, for they hoard coin like misers yet spend it upon unseen servers in distant realms, feeding ghostly machines that hum day and night without sup or sleep.  

And though they boasted of "150% net retention," I saw no fields of wheat nor herds of kine—only phantom customers multiplying like loaves and fishes through arts I dare not name, lest the Inquisition take notice.
